scenarios.description: Mega Charizard X uses its Mega forme base Attack of 130, not the base forme's 84, in damage calculations
scenarios.note: Mega Charizard X base Attack is 130 (not 84). Damage calc uses Mega form stats. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts MegaEvolution stat override

scenarios.description: Mega Evolution is permanent for the battle — a Pokemon cannot revert to base form by switching out (ERRATA #18)
scenarios.note: Once Mega-Evolved, the Pokemon retains its Mega form even after switching out and back in. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts MegaEvolution persistence — ERRATA #18

scenarios.description: Parental Bond's second hit deals 50% of the first hit's damage in Gen 6 (changed to 25% in Gen 7 — ERRATA #12)
scenarios.note: Parental Bond second hit = 50% in Gen 6 (not 25%). Changed to 25% in Gen 7 (ERRATA #12). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts ParentalBond Gen6

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 2x damage to Dragon-type Pokemon (ERRATA #11)
scenarios.note: Fairy is super-effective vs Dragon. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart — ERRATA #11

scenarios.description: Dragon-type moves deal 0 damage to Fairy-type Pokemon — Fairy is immune to Dragon
scenarios.note: Fairy is immune to Dragon-type moves. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart — ERRATA #11

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 2x damage to Fighting-type Pokemon
scenarios.note: Fairy is super-effective vs Fighting. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 2x damage to Dark-type Pokemon
scenarios.note: Fairy is super-effective vs Dark. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 0.5x damage to Fire-type Pokemon
scenarios.note: Fairy is not very effective vs Fire. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 0.5x damage to Poison-type Pokemon
scenarios.note: Fairy is not very effective vs Poison. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Fairy-type moves deal 0.5x damage to Steel-type Pokemon (Steel resists Fairy)
scenarios.note: Steel resists Fairy (0.5x). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Knock Off deals 1.5x damage when the target holds an item (introduced Gen 6 — ERRATA #25)
scenarios.note: Knock Off 1.5x damage boost introduced in Gen 6 (ERRATA #25). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts KnockOff Gen6

scenarios.description: Consecutive Protect uses: success rate triples-down per use in Gen 6 (1/3, 1/9, 1/27...) — ERRATA #9
scenarios.note: Gen 6 changed Protect consecutive rate from halving to tripling. Attempt 1=100%, 2=33.3%, 3=11.1%, 4=3.7%. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts ProtectCheck Gen6 — ERRATA #9

scenarios.description: Gale Wings gives +1 priority to ALL Flying-type moves in Gen 6 with no HP condition (ERRATA #13)
scenarios.note: Gale Wings Gen 6: +1 priority for all Flying moves regardless of HP. The HP condition (HP must be full) was added in Gen 7 (ERRATA #13). Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts GaleWings Gen6

scenarios.description: Pixilate, Refrigerate, Aerilate, and Galvanize convert Normal-type moves to their type and boost power by 1.2x
scenarios.note: Pixilate (and other -ate abilities): Normal moves become the ability's type and gain 1.2x power in Gen 6. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Pixilate Gen6

scenarios.description: Electric Terrain boosts Electric-type moves used by grounded Pokemon by 1.5x
scenarios.note: Electric Terrain: 1.5x boost to Electric moves for grounded Pokemon. Terrain introduced Gen 6. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts ElectricTerrain

scenarios.description: Terrain moves (Electric/Psychic/Misty/Grassy Terrain) last 5 turns by default, 8 turns with Terrain Extender
scenarios.note: Terrain was introduced in Gen 6. Duration: 5 turns base, 8 with Terrain Extender. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Terrain duration Gen6

scenarios.description: Sticky Web lowers Speed by 1 stage for grounded Pokemon switching into the field (introduced Gen 6)
scenarios.note: Sticky Web was introduced in Gen 6. Grounded incoming Pokemon lose -1 Speed. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts StickyWeb

scenarios.description: Oblivious prevents the bearer from being targeted by Taunt and Encore in Gen 6
scenarios.note: Oblivious blocks Taunt and Encore starting in Gen 6. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Oblivious Gen6

scenarios.description: Aegislash switches between Shield (defensive) and Blade (offensive) form based on the move used
scenarios.note: Stance Change: Aegislash shifts to Blade Form when using an attacking move, Shield Form when using King's Shield. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts StanceChange

scenarios.description: Mega Stones cannot be removed by Knock Off, Thief, Trick, or Switcheroo
scenarios.note: Mega Stones are immune to item removal. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts MegaStone item removal immunity

scenarios.description: In Gen 6, ability-summoned weather (Drizzle/Drought/Sand Stream/Snow Warning) lasts 5 turns (changed from Gen 5's permanent weather)
scenarios.note: Gen 6 added 5-turn duration to ability-summoned weather (8 turns with weather rock items). This is a significant nerf from Gen 5. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 weather duration

scenarios.description: Defiant raises Attack by +2 stages when any stat is lowered by an opponent (including Intimidate)
scenarios.note: Defiant: +2 Attack on any stat drop from opponents, including Intimidate. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Defiant

scenarios.description: Poison-type moves deal 2x damage to Fairy-type Pokemon (Fairy's weakness to Poison)
scenarios.note: Poison is super-effective vs Fairy. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart

scenarios.description: Gen 6 has 18 types: the 17 from prior gens plus the newly added Fairy type
scenarios.note: Fairy type was added in Gen 6, bringing the total type count to 18. Source: Showdown sim/battle-actions.ts Gen6 type chart
